items.title: 'Dancefloor's packed, but the bar isn't busy': How night-life is changing

children.content: This weekend will be the first since the sudden closure of seven Hobart venues. A manager of another bar says he doesn't know what to expect.

gallery.altText: Two young women smiling and dancing

thumbnail.altText: Two young women smiling and dancing

items.title: Push to invest in early intervention as calls to police surge

children.content: A review of Tasmania Police finds calls for help have risen by 40,000 annually over the past decade, fuelling demands for investment in early intervention support services.

image.altText: A close-up of police hats on laps as police officer sit at a memorial service.

items.title: Hobart 'Stadium 2.0' developer's company in liquidation with unpaid super, millions in debts

children.content: Tasmanian developer Dean Coleman's company, which was involved in the rejected 'Stadium 2.0' plan for Hobart, is in liquidation. He's now involved in a separate development proposal at Regatta Point.

image.altText: An artist's impression of a round silver stadium jutting into the water, surrounded by green space, with Hobart city behind.

items.title: In troubled times, this unruly seal brings joy to the world

items.title: Hanson says Taylor should stop 'denigrating' One Nation

children.content: Pauline Hanson says Angus Taylor needs to stop "denigrating" One Nation, after the opposition leader delivered his strongest rebuke so far of the right-wing party. Follow live.

items.title: The meteorite at the centre of a 'culture war' between the UK and Australia

children.content: The fight to claim a 3.5-tonne space rock didn't just kickstart a publicity battle that divided an empire — it also left a gaping hole in culture. Centuries on, the question remains: Who owned it?

image.altText: A meteorite with a tail of flame burning behind it hits the Earth with a purple sky above.

articles.title: First Indigenous Military Cross recipient discovered by War Memorial

children.content: Captain Sidney Leonard Morse's achievements would have remained hidden were it not for a chance discovery by a volunteer historian collating family trees at the Australian War Memorial.

articles.title: Cultural hub in Tennant Creek celebrates Warumungu culture for NAIDOC

children.content: Hundreds of people walked through the streets of Tennant Creek today as part of a NAIDOC march, finishing at the official reopening of the town's major First Nations cultural hub.

items.title: Girls married off as a climate change 'coping strategy'

children.content: Climate change is now believed to be a leading contributor to more frequent and younger nuptials, with 12 million girls tipped to become child brides this year.

image.altText: A young woman holds up her hands covered in henna to the camera.

items.title: How China's military goals in the Pacific are rapidly progressing

children.content: China's firing of a dummy warhead was the first time in two years it tested its long-range missile arsenal — and the first time ever it had launched such a rocket from a nuclear-powered submarine.

image.altText: A rocket launches from the sea leaving smoke in its wake.

items.title: 'Risk of death': Snowy Hydro worker's narrow escape from falling truck

items.title: Proposed laws to extend maximum detention to 72 hours for mental health check

items.title: Getting guernseys reverses fortunes of one of the most remote footy clubs

children.content: The Fregon Bulldogs are among the nation's most remote Australian Rules football clubs, and for several seasons were forced to play in casual clothes. But the club has secured new guernseys — and the change in uniform has led to a change in form.

image.altText: The new design of the guernsey of the Fregon Bulldogs football club.

items.title: Three corrections officers stood down as review launched into prisoner escape

children.content: The Northern Territory Corrections Department has stood down three of its workers with pay over a prisoner escape on Sunday that triggered a more than 48-hour manhunt.

items.title: Gout Gout ruled out of World Under-20 athletics titles after training injury
